contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 40
- Description: Hook the batch-QR pipeline into Edgar (repoint QR_CODE_GENERATION webhook to the 1N6o00 processor)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/tokenomics/pull/370
  
  Details:
  Diagnosed and fixed the async Edgar->GAS batch-QR pipeline (per QR_GENERATION_DAO_CLIENT_POSTMORTEM.md), which was broken end-to-end. dao_client truesight-dao-batch-qr-generator emits [BATCH QR CODE REQUEST]; Edgar dispatch.py routes it to DAO_PROTOCOL_WEBHOOK_QR_CODE_GENERATION ?action=processQRCodeGenerationTelegramLogs. Root causes: (1) the 1N6o00 processor web app served Page Not Found because appsscript.json lacked a webapp block (fixed in tokenomics #370; redeployed the existing AKfycbxn3siu deployment to v8). (2) Edgar's DAO_PROTOCOL_WEBHOOK_QR_CODE_GENERATION pointed at the wrong GAS deployment (a 1MnAs query service AKfycbyGD with actions list/search/generate only, no processQRCodeGenerationTelegramLogs), so every dispatch returned Invalid action and nothing processed. Repointed it directly at the 1N6o00 processor on dao_protocol_nelanco (.env backed up, service restarted, var verified in process environ). Marker mismatch from the 2026-06-20 postmortem was already resolved in the 2026-06-22 code merge. Documented the resolution + remaining upstream data-quality gap (repackaging ingest doesn't set isSerializable/farm cols) in the postmortem and CONTEXT_UPDATES.md.
- Contributor(s): Claude Anthropic
- TDG Issued: 66.67
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
